explanation: The natural language states that a mode switch from PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) to PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) should occur if (and only if) the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window (apnea lag time). The atomic proposition 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' expresses that the breath timer has exceeded, i.e., apnea is detected. The requirement does not mention any other conditions triggering the transition; thus, this is the unique trigger. The second part concerns target parameters for the backup mode, but these are not directly encoded in the atomic propositions and thus not formalizable here. The proper structured NL is thus: "whenever breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy PCVModeSelected". This means each time the apnea window is exceeded (no breath triggered in time), the ventilator shall switch to PCV mode immediately.
decision2_substring: if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision3_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur

explanation: The requirement states that the ventilator should switch from Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode to Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode if the patient does not trigger a breath within the apnea trigger window. In the provided atomic propositions, 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' corresponds to the patient not triggering a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window, and 'PCVModeSelected' corresponds to mode switching. The property must be structured such that whenever the trigger timeout occurs (i.e., patient doesn't breathe within allowed lag), the switch to PCV mode is made, and, after the switch, certain parameters must be set ('RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP'). The chosen structure expresses that whenever 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' holds, a mode switch to PCV is performed, and that after this switch, backup mode parameters are set. This captures both the triggering event and the mandated parameter settings, using provided atomic propositions.
decision1_substring: occur if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision2_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur
decision3_substring: The switch shall occur with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode.
output_structured_natural_language: whenever breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, upon PCVModeSelected, always satisfy RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP

explanation: The given requirement stipulates that the system must switch from PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode to PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode if the patient does not trigger a breath within an allowable time window, specifically referred to as the apnea trigger window. Looking at the available atomic propositions, 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' indicates that the patient has not taken a breath within the apnea lag time, which serves as the trigger for the mode switch. 'PCVModeSelected' indicates the command to switch to PCV mode is issued, and 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP' respectively confirm that the respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio for apnea backup mode are set. The structured NL thus triggers, whenever the apnea window has been exceeded ('breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime'), a switch command to PCV is issued and the backup parameters are set. The selected template expresses this clearly: upon the detection of no patient breath in the window, immediately satisfy the conditions of switching modes and parameters, consistently with the intent and the mapped atomic propositions.
decision1_substring: if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision3_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur ... with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode.
output_structured_natural_language: upon breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy PCVModeSelected & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP

explanation: The requirement specifies that, if the patient fails to trigger a breath within the allowable apnea lag time, a transition from Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) to Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode must occur. The switch to PCV mode must occur with specific ventilation parameters: respiratory rate (RR), target inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio defined for the apnea backup mode. This is captured by monitoring for the condition 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' (i.e., the patient fails to trigger a breath within the allowed time), and then performing the PCV mode switch—by ensuring 'PCVModeSelected', and setting the apnea backup parameters: 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP'. The structured NL expresses that whenever this condition occurs, these mode switches and parameter changes must happen immediately.
decision1_substring: if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision3_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur ... The switch shall occur with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode.
output_structured_natural_language: whenever breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy PCVModeSelected & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP

explanation: The input requirement specifies that a transition from Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode to Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode should occur if the patient does not trigger a breath within the apnea trigger window, which can be inferred from the atomic proposition 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime'. The second part about switching with specific parameters is covered by the atomic propositions for respiratory rate (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), target inspiratory pressure (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and I:E ratio (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). Thus, the translation uses the 'upon' template to capture the event of apnea (no breath within apnea lag time) and the property that, upon that event, certain PCV backup parameters must be set. The structured property covers both triggering (decision2: upon breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime) and the outcome (decision3: immediately satisfy all necessary parameter assignments).
decision2_substring: shall occur if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision3_substring: The switch shall occur with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode.
output_structured_natural_language: upon breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy PCVModeSelected & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP

explanation: The input requirement specifies that a mode switch from PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) to PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) should happen if the patient does not trigger a breath within a specified apnea window. Based on the available atomic propositions, 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' captures the 'no breath triggered within the apnea trigger window', and 'PCVModeSelected' captures the initiation of the mode switch to PCV. The additional requirement that RR, inspiratory pressure and I:E parameters are set for apnea backup are represented by the propositions 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP'. Thus, whenever the condition 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' holds, the system must immediately set the PCV mode and the apnea backup parameters as described. This is captured by the structure: 'whenever breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy (PCVModeSelected & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP)'.
decision1_substring: if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision3_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur ... The switch shall occur with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode.
output_structured_natural_language: whenever breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy PCVModeSelected & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP

explanation: The requirement specifies that if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window, the system shall transition from Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) mode to Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) mode with mode parameters (RR, target inspiratory pressure, and I:E) set for apnea backup. The atomic proposition 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' represents the absence of a breathing trigger (apnea) and 'PCVModeSelected' denotes that the PCV mode transition is initiated. The remaining requirement about setting parameters is understood to be part of the PCV mode selection process (as no additional AP directly encodes parameter setting). Therefore, the property is captured by stating: Whenever the apnea condition (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime) occurs, PCV mode transition (PCVModeSelected) shall eventually be triggered.
decision1_substring: if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision3_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur

explanation: The requirement states a transition (switch) from PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode to PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode must occur IF the patient does not trigger a breath within the time defined by the apnea trigger window. From the atomic propositions, 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' directly corresponds to the patient not breathing within the allowed time. The switch should set respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E to their apnea backup values. We assume switching to PCV mode is captured by 'PCVModeSelected' (indicates the machine is commanded to switch to PCV) and additional settings are 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP'. The core property is that whenever the condition 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' holds (apnea window exceeded), the controller must immediately switch to PCV mode and apply backup settings. This is best captured as: whenever 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime', immediately satisfy ('PCVModeSelected' & 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP' & 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP' & 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP').
decision1_substring: if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision3_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur ... The switch shall occur with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode.
output_structured_natural_language: whenever breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy PCVModeSelected & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP

explanation: The natural language requirement specifies that a transition from PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) to PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) should occur if the patient does not trigger a breath within the 'apnea trigger window'. Since 'apnea trigger window' reasoning in atomic propositions corresponds to 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime', this atomic proposition expresses that condition. The transition involves switching the mode, which is represented by 'PCVModeSelected'. Additionally, it is required that, after the transition, the parameters for respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio are those defined for the apnea backup mode. In the atomic propositions, 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP' (for inspiratory pressure), and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP' (for I:E) together ensure this. Thus, whenever the apnea lag time is reached (no breath), the system must immediately select PCV mode with all the proper backup parameters set for apnea backup mode.
decision1_substring: if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window
decision3_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur ... The switch shall occur with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode.
output_structured_natural_language: whenever breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy PCVModeSelected & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP

explanation: The requirement specifies that the switch from Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) to Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) backup mode must occur if the patient does not initiate a breath within the defined apnea window. According to the atomic propositions, 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' indicates that the patient has not triggered a breath within the allowable time, and 'PCVModeSelected' indicates a command to switch to PCV mode. The subsequent settings (respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio) to their apnea backup defined values can be encoded using 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP'. Therefore, the structured NL property captures that upon 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime', the system must: 1) Switch to PCV mode, 2) Set the respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio to their specified apnea backup mode values.
decision1_substring: The transition from PSV to PCV shall occur if the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window.
decision3_substring: The switch shall occur with respiratory rate, target inspiratory pressure and I:E defined for the apnea backup mode.
output_structured_natural_language: upon breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime, immediately satisfy PCVModeSelected & RR_equalTo_RR_AP & P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP & ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP
